models.code:
class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customer'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    credit_limit = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
    balance = Column(Decimal, nullable=False, default=0)
models.description: Model for Customer entity with balance and credit limit
models.name: Customer

models.code:
class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customer.id'))
    notes = Column(String)
    date_shipped = Column(Date, nullable=True)
    amount_total = Column(Decimal, nullable=False, default=0)
models.description: Model for Order entity with relation to Customer
models.name: Order

models.code:
class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'item'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('order.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('product.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    unit_price = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
    amount = Column(Decimal, nullable=False, default=0)
models.description: Model for Item entity with references to Order and Product
models.name: Item

models.code:
class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'product'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    unit_price = Column(Decimal, nullable=False)
models.name: Product

rules.name: CustomerBalanceConstraint
rules.description: Check customer's balance against their credit limit.
rules.code:
Rule.constraint(validate=Customer,
    as_condition=lambda row: row.balance <= row.credit_limit,
    error_msg="Customer balance ({row.balance}) exceeds credit limit ({row.credit_limit})")

rules.name: CustomerBalanceSumRule
rules.code: Rule.sum(derive=Customer.balance, as_sum_of=Order.amount_total, where=lambda row: row.date_shipped is None)

rules.name: ItemAmountFormulaRule
rules.description: Compute Item amount as quantity * unit_price.

rules.name: OrderKafkaEventRule
rules.description: Send Order data to Kafka if date_shipped is not None.
rules.code:
Rule.after_flush_row_event(on_class=Order, calling=kafka_producer.send_row_to_kafka,
    if_condition=lambda row: row.date_shipped is not None,
    with_args={"topic": "order_shipping"})

customer4 = Customer(name="Alice Williams", credit_limit=20000, balance=0)

# Create test products

product2 = Product(name="Monitor", unit_price=200)
product3 = Product(name="Mouse", unit_price=20)
product4 = Product(name="Keyboard", unit_price=50)

# Create test orders
order1 = Order(customer_id=1, notes="First order", date_shipped=date(2023, 1, 10), amount_total=0)
order2 = Order(customer_id=2, notes="Gift", date_shipped=None, amount_total=0)
order3 = Order(customer_id=1, notes="Express delivery", date_shipped=None, amount_total=0)
order4 = Order(customer_id=3, notes="Bulk order", date_shipped=date(2023, 1, 12), amount_total=0)

# Create test items

item2 = Item(order_id=1, product_id=2, quantity=2, unit_price=200, amount=400)

item4 = Item(order_id=2, product_id=4, quantity=10, unit_price=50, amount=500)
